title: Windows Command Line, PowerShell Boundaries, and Client Networking
description: A remote technician receives copied command output from a Windows client and must diagnose path, process, file, identity, address, name, route, firewall, and policy state without running broad repair commands. Learners cumulatively produce safe Windows command evidence transcript through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish cmd, PowerShell, executable, built-in command, alias, script, pipeline, object, text, quoting, variable, path, privilege, and execution-policy behavior.
Choose non-destructive commands to inspect directories, files, permissions, environment, system identity, processes, services, tasks, disks, and integrity before repair.
Inspect adapter, address, DHCP, DNS, gateway, route, neighbor, proxy, VPN, profile, sharing, and firewall state with provenance.
